description: From the manufacturer As of July 2020, packaging may vary to accommodate an increased pack size. Green-finger friendly slug control Keep your lawn, garden and outdoor areas free from unwanted pests with the comprehensive range of welfare-friendly and poison-free solutions from Defenders. Effective in both wet and dry weather Helps retain soil moisture reducing the need for watering Rich in natural nutrients essential for plant growth The pellets are held together with sheep wool and biodegradable protein fibres Slugs Away Wool Pellets - 3.5 Litre from Defenders All-natural barrier keeps snails and slugs away from plants – an effective alternative to chemical slug pellets Slugs Away from Defenders is a non-toxic slug and snail deterrent. Suitable for use in gardens and allotments, once wet it forms a natural, mat-like barrier protecting vulnerable plants from damage by slugs and snails. The wool fibres irritate the foot of the slug forcing them away from the protected area. Formulated using natural ingredients it is safe for use around children and animals including birds, dogs and hedgehogs and is effective in both wet and dry weather conditions. Poison-free. Wool-based pellets naturally deter slugs and snails without using chemical controls. Suitable for organic gardening, with natural slow-release of plant nutrients from biodegradable protein fibres. Acts as a weed suppressant and reduces the need for watering. Part of the Slugs Away range from Defenders. Product Highlights Organic, poison-free Formulated using natural ingredients and is effective in both wet and dry weather conditions. Biodegradeable Pellets naturally degrade without trace or harm. Easy to use Suitable for fruit, flowers and vegetables. Place in a 10cm band around the base of plants and water-in. Organic wool pellets form a mulch that doesn't wash or blow away. Child and pet safe Poison free, safe for use around children and animals including birds, dogs and hedgehogs. Slimy Trails These gastropods are a common feature among British gardens, research suggesting around 200 slugs can be found per cubic metre. Both love wet conditions and so are often more active after rainfall and can be found hiding under planters and pots where soil and gravel may be damp. Snails tend to hibernate throughout winter so are less active in this season but slugs continue their habits in temperatures as low as 5 degrees, munching on veggies almost year-round. As nocturnal creatures that shy away from the sun, you’re more likely to spot them at night-time. A sure sign of either in your garden are their silvery trails and nibbled leaves. They don’t discriminate and consume young and decaying growth alike across vegetables, seedlings, and ornamental plants. Some slugs carry lungworm, which is a severe problem for dogs. Plant defence It’s recommended that a slug or snail will need to be moved at least 20 metres away from your garden to force them to become disorientated and lose their way back as their homing instincts cover short distances only. They can stretch up to 20 times their size to fit through small gaps so barriers must be continuous. Raking over soil and removing fallen leaves can expose slug eggs to birds for them to eat. Growing plants with hairy stems or strong-smelling scents, such as lavender or rosemary, which are unappealing will deter them. Heading out into the garden at night with a torch to manually remove these gastropods can be effective but highly time-consuming. Defenders have a range of products for easier management. Slugs Away The range of Slugs Away products from Defenders covers every angle of slug and snail protection. Every product is organic and biodegradable, suitable for use in areas frequented by pets or children. Use the Wool Mat with added growth benefits, such as weed suppressor, soil insulator and frost protector. Easy to apply, the mats come in two sizes and can be cut to size. The Plant Protection Guard creates a sturdy lip that can’t be traversed and the Barrier Tape and copper Band creates an uncomfortable barrier for slugs and snails to travel over.
